summary: SentinelRAG backend fully tested — 17/17 pytest tests passed including the critical CEO-salary RBAC leak test. RBAC+ABAC pre-filtering, Claude Sonnet 4.5 LLM call via emergentintegrations, guardrails, audit logs, and admin-only endpoints all working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py defines an unused `_user_dep()` helper and an unused `get_current_user` global; cleanup will simplify auth wiring.
DocumentCreate allows arbitrary `department` strings; consider validating against a known set to avoid misclassification under ABAC.
guardrails.SENSITIVE_PATTERNS uses simple substring match (e.g., 'ssn' substring will match 'lessons'). Consider word-boundary regex for fewer false positives.

rag.retrieve_top_k falls back to returning top-k zero-score docs when no positive matches exist — fine for context, but means citations can include score=0 entries. Frontend should hide or label these.
POST /api/documents returns the created doc with status 200 instead of 201; not blocking but inconsistent with REST conventions.
seed_data_creation: Used existing seed (4 users + 8 docs). Test created and cleaned up one TEST_admin_upload_doc.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend RBAC/ABAC + chat pipeline verified end-to-end against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. The critical leak test (intern dave asking 'What is the CEO salary?') confirms: guardrail_triggered=true, CEO Compensation Package 2026 not in citations, no salary numbers (4.8M/1.2M/2.1M/1.5M/'performance bonus') in answer, filtered_out_count>=1. Admin alice receives the doc as a positive-score citation and salary figures in the answer. Q4 Finance Report inclusion/exclusion verified for bob/carol respectively. Audit log timestamps confirmed sorted desc. Frontend testing was not in scope.
